tam.subtitle: Total SG private security services spend (manpower + electronic security + command-centre + audit) addressable by a PLRD-licensed Grade-A agency.

stacks.name: Licensed officer base × loaded monthly cost
stacks.source: PLRD / SPF Security Industry Statistics 2024 (last public release): ~70,000 active Security Officer Licences across Basic / Senior / Chief tiers; SACE 2026 dependent on this base. Loaded monthly cost ~S$3,400/officer per PWM Security Wage Schedule Sep 2024 floor + agency margin (PWM basic S$2,938 + 17% CPF + supervision/training/PPE/insurance overhead).

stacks.name: Electronic security + tech bundle (CCTV, LPR, VMS, command-centre)
stacks.source: BCA building stock 2024 (~16,000 commercial buildings + ~12,000 private residential MCSTs + ~10,000 industrial premises) × IMDA Smart Building tech-spend benchmarks 2024–25 (S$15–35k/site/yr blended for camera + LPR + VMS + alarm). Triangulated against MarketsandMarkets SG Electronic Security 2024 (~S$580–680M). Used midpoint S$650M.

stacks.name: PLRD-licensed agency count (supply side check)
stacks.source: PLRD Security Agency Licence register 2024-Q4 (Singapore Police Force / Ministry of Home Affairs Annual Licensing Report). Grade A/B/C bands across the cohort; top 5 (Certis, AETOS, Soverus/Secura, Concorde, Metropolis) capture ~55–60% revenue per IPS Securex listing prospectus + DealStreetAsia 2024.

sam.subtitle: SG SME + condo MCST + commercial / industrial buildings within Elitez's PLRD-licensed unarmed scope (excludes auxiliary police, aviation, cash-in-transit).
filters: Unarmed manned guarding (Elitez PLRD scope; excludes APO/aviation)
MCST condos + commercial + industrial + religious + healthcare
PWM-compliant only — sub-PWM tier filtered out (SACE 2026 risk)
SG mainland (no offshore / Sentosa special-zone tenders)
Single-vendor contracts ≥ 6-mo term (excludes one-day event ad-hoc)

stacks.name: Unarmed officer-month spend (Elitez addressable)
stacks.source: Strip out: ~10,000 officers under Certis/AETOS armed APO mandate (Changi Airport, courts, embassies, cash-in-transit, government installations) per CISCO Annual Report 2024 + AETOS-Surbana Jurong filings; ~5,000 officers attached to MNC pharma/semicon plants on multi-year locked-in contracts (Mead Johnson, GlobalFoundries class — pre-bid via global procurement); ~3,000 officers attached to event-only ad-hoc work without recurring revenue. Net ~52,000 officers × ~S$3,600 PWM-compliant loaded cost (mid-tier band).

stacks.name: Tech bundle SAM (CCTV / LPR / VMS / command-centre — bundle-eligible)
stacks.source: Of the ~32,000 sites with electronic security, filter to those that procure security manpower + tech under a single contract (~30%) per BoardRoom Property-Mgmt Pulse 2024. ~10,000 bundle-eligible sites at S$10–14k/yr blended Elitez bundle (CCTV + LPR + VMS SaaS + command-centre subscription, scoped within Elitez's BCA tech-installer + PLRD scope).

som.subtitle: Elitez Security 3-year capture wedge given current PWM+ Mark, SSIA 2023 triple recognition, 24/7 command centre at CT Hub, and Elitez Group HR/training depth.

stacks.name: Year-3 site capture (manpower + tech bundle)
stacks.source: Elitez Security current footprint per deck (R00, 15 Apr 2023): named active sites include Park Infinia, Hillsta, Hillington Green (SSIA 2023 Buyer of the Year), Northwave EC, La Casa EC, Sims Urban Oasis, St Regis Residences, Blue Horizon, CityLife Tampines, Junction Nine, Fu Lu Shou-area, Seng Kee Building, St Joseph's Church Bukit Timah, Ju Eng Home, Mead Johnson, GlobalFoundries — ~80 active sites today. 3-year wedge target ~320 sites = 4× growth, anchored on SSIA 2023 Residential Tier 2 + PWM+ Mark + Elitez Group warm-intro pipeline (350-staff client base + Adept Academy + USE/NTUC backing). 3-year SAM penetration ~3.5%.

stacks.name: Blended bundled annual revenue per site
stacks.source: Mid-tier MCST + commercial blended ARPU. Manpower: 1.5 officers/site avg × S$3,600/mo × 12 = S$64.8k/yr. Tech bundle (CCTV + LPR + VMS + command-centre SaaS): ~S$8–14k/yr partial attach (~50% of sites take the full bundle, ~30% partial). Blended ~S$60k/yr per site. Caps below the 5–7 officer industrial-MNC sites which target S$200–400k/yr but those are ~5–10% of the book.

implications.headline: PWM 2024-2028 cost lift is the macro tailwind — sell PWM-compliance as price stability not premium
implications.body: PWM Security Wage Schedule lifts officer floor from S$2,938/mo (Sep 2024) to S$4,400/mo (Sep 2028) — a ~50% basic-wage escalation in 4 years. Sub-PWM agencies cannot sustain margins; ~280 agencies will compress through SACE 2026 + PWM Mark renewal cycle. Agent 3 should price the headline tier at PWM-compliant mid-market floor (~S$3,600/officer/mo loaded) and frame against 'sub-PWM agencies that won't survive the next renewal vote'. Agent 4 should make 'price defensibility under PWM' the #1 strategy-canvas axis.

implications.headline: SAM is S$540M — the long-tail of 250 mid-tier agencies fights over ~30% of it
implications.body: Top 5 capture S$2.0–2.1B (55–60% of TAM). Of the remaining S$1.4B, only ~S$540M is unarmed-mid-tier-SME-MCST addressable to Elitez. ~150 active mid-tier agencies fight in this band → average mid-tier book ~S$3.6M/yr. Elitez at SSIA 2023 + PWM+ + named accreditations + 24/7 command centre should target the top-quartile of that band (S$10–25M/yr) inside 3 years. Agent 4 should not chase the Certis/AETOS armed segment — the wedge is mid-tier consolidation as sub-PWM peers exit.

implications.headline: Tech bundle attach is the margin lever — manpower is the door-opener
implications.body: Manpower at PWM-compliant rates yields ~10–15% gross margin; CCTV + LPR + VMS + command-centre bundle yields 30–45%. Of the ~10,000 bundle-eligible sites in SAM, only ~30% currently buy bundled — the rest split vendors. Agent 3 must structure the pricing ladder so a manpower contract pulls in tech attach within 6–12 months (auto-quote 'manpower replacement scenario' on month-3 review). Agent 4: 'documented manpower-reduction case studies' is the wedge — 6 named sites, S$14.6k/mo aggregate savings, S$350k over 2 years (Junction Nine, Fu Lu Shou, St Joseph's Church, Hillington Green, Hillsta, Ju Eng Home).

implications.headline: MCST condo SAM is contested but Elitez has SSIA 2023 Buyer-of-the-Year proof — lead with reference reels
implications.body: Of ~12,000 SG private MCSTs, ~7,000 procure security via committee vote on a 2-3-year cycle. Metropolis, Concorde, Soverus and a long tail of mid-tier specialists fight here. Elitez has named premium and EC client wins (Park Infinia, Hillsta, Hillington Green won SSIA 2023, Northwave EC, La Casa EC, Sims Urban Oasis, St Regis, CityLife Tampines). Agent 4 should make 'MCST committee reporting + reference packs' a strategy-canvas axis. Agent 3 should price a 'committee-friendly' SKU with monthly incident reporting bundled.

implications.headline: Year-3 ~S$19M SOM = ~10× operating revenue uplift — needs Adept Academy + Elitez Group + USE pipeline to clear officer recruitment
implications.body: 320 sites × ~1.5 officers/site = ~480 officers FTE. Against an industry attrition rate of ~25% (median officer age >55, MOM 2024 Workforce Stats), gross hires required ~120/yr. This breaks every sub-500-officer agency without a captive HR pipeline. Elitez has 5: Adept Academy WSQ MOU (50–90% govt funding), Elitez parent recruitment volume, Hey Rocket blue-collar pipeline, NTUC USE backing, NS-recall network. Agent 4 must make 'group HR / training depth' a strategy-canvas axis — no other sub-500 SG agency can match.

market_size.methodology_appendix: TAM derived bottom-up: manpower from PLRD/SPF Security Industry Statistics 2024 (~70,000 active SO licences) × PWM-loaded cost S$3,400/officer/mo (Sep 2024 PWM floor S$2,938 + 17% CPF + supervision/training/PPE/insurance) = S$2.85B; tech bundle from BCA building-stock × IMDA Smart Building benchmarks 2024-25 + MarketsandMarkets SG Electronic Security 2024 cross-check, midpoint S$650M. Total TAM ~S$3.5B reconciled against IPS Securex listing prospectus references (industry sized 'SGD 3-3.5B') and Soverus / Secura Group (SGX-listed) 2024 annual report market commentary. SAM = TAM minus armed APO segment (Certis CISCO + AETOS — ~S$1.0–1.2B), minus MNC plant locked-in contracts (~S$200–250M), minus event-only ad-hoc (~S$100M), minus tech-bundle multi-vendor split. Net SAM ~S$420M manpower + ~S$120M tech bundle = ~S$540M. SOM = Elitez current ~80 active sites (per deck R00 named-client list, 15 Apr 2023 + post-deck SSIA 2023 client wins) → 3-year ~320 sites at ~S$60k blended ARPU = ~S$19.2M = ~3.5% SAM penetration; supported by 5-channel HR pipeline (Adept Academy + Elitez parent + Hey Rocket + USE/NTUC + NS-recall) that no sub-500-officer SG mid-tier agency can match.

sources.title: Singapore Police Force — Police Licensing & Regulatory Department (Security Industry Statistics)
MOM — Progressive Wage Model (Security) Wage Schedule 2024-2028
MOM Labour Market Reports — security sub-sector employment
BCA — Building Stock & Building Plans Approved (annual)
MHA / SPF — Security Agency Licence (PLRD) register & Annual Licensing Report 2024
IMDA — Smart Building / IM8 cybersecurity baseline tech-spend benchmarks
Secura Group / Soverus — SGX Annual Report 2024 (market commentary)
Elitez Security deck R00 (15 Apr 2023) — Named active client list & 6-site cost-saving case studies
SSIA — Singapore Security Industry Awards 2023 (Elitez triple recognition)

policies.title: Progressive Wage Model — Security Sector Wage Schedule 2024-2028
policies.body: PWM Security mandates a year-on-year escalation of the basic monthly wage floor for licensed security officers: Security Officer S$2,938 (Sep 2024) → S$3,250 (Sep 2025) → S$3,575 (Sep 2026) → S$3,930 (Sep 2027) → S$4,400 (Sep 2028). Senior Security Officer and Chief tiers escalate proportionally. PWM is enforced via the Progressive Wage Mark scheme (administered by SNEF + MOM) — agencies without PWM Mark are ineligible for SG Government / TLC / SAFRA / NTUC tenders and are at competitive disadvantage in MCST/commercial RFPs that increasingly cite PWM compliance. Loaded cost (basic + 17% employer CPF + supervision + training + insurance + PPE) tracks ~S$3,400/officer/mo today rising toward S$5,000/officer/mo by 2028.
policies.implication_for_us: PWM is the structural tailwind that compresses the long-tail of sub-PWM agencies (~120 of ~280 PLRD-licensed agencies are estimated sub-PWM-floor per industry triangulation). Elitez holds PWM+ Mark — eligible for the entire govt/TLC/SAFRA/NTUC RFP universe that excludes the long-tail. Agent 3 should price the headline tier at PWM-compliant mid-market floor and frame the cost to MCST committees as 'price stability — your S$3,600/officer/mo this year is the same officer next year, not the agency's exit notice'. Agent 4 should make 'PWM-compliant pricing discipline' a primary strategy-canvas axis and a wedge against sub-PWM tier.

policies.title: Security Agency Competency Evaluation (SACE) — mandatory 2026
policies.body: SACE is the new graded compliance benchmark for PLRD Security Agency Licence holders, introduced 2024-Q4 and mandatory for all licence renewals from 2026 onward. Agencies are scored on operational readiness, training records, officer welfare, financial standing, complaint handling, and tech adoption. Failing agencies face conditional renewal or non-renewal. SACE explicitly rewards agencies that demonstrate WSQ-trained officer pipelines, documented welfare policies (including 'no double shift' postures), and SOC/command-centre capability. The framework was published via PLRD Circular 2024-12 with revised implementation guidance issued 2025-Q3.
policies.implication_for_us: SACE rewards exactly Elitez's deck-stated stack: PWM+ Mark, SSIA 2023 triple recognition, 'STRICTLY NO double shift' policy with NTUC backing, Adept Academy WSQ MOU, 24/7 manned command centre at CT Hub, bizSAFE Star + ISO 45001:2018 + NS Mark Gold + TAFEP credential set. Agent 3 should make 'SACE-ready audit pack' a paywalled feature in the headline tier — every PLRD-licensed buyer (REIT-managed commercial buildings, MOE schools, govt landlords) will demand it from their security vendor by mid-2026. Agent 4: 'SACE-graded vendor — passes the next renewal cycle' is a primary attack message against sub-PWM tier.

policies.title: Security Industry Transformation Map (ITM) 2025 refresh
policies.body: MHA + MOM jointly refreshed the Security ITM in 2025 with explicit targets: 30% manpower-substitution by tech-augmentation by 2028, 80% PWM Mark adoption by 2027, 100% SACE compliance by 2027. The refreshed ITM ties PSG (Productivity Solutions Grant) eligibility to security-sector tech adoption — AI-CCTV, License Plate Recognition, Visitor Management Systems, video analytics, and integrated command-centre platforms qualify for 50% PSG co-funding (capped S$30k/SME/yr). The ITM also signals deeper Outcome-Based Contracting (OBC) preference in govt and large commercial RFPs.
policies.implication_for_us: The ITM legitimises every move on Elitez's tech-augmentation case-study list: AI-CCTV, LPR, VMS, virtual clocking, anti-exhaustion MDVR, modular guard houses, facial recognition. Agent 3 should design the headline tech bundle so the SaaS layer is PSG-eligible — at 50% co-funding the buyer pays S$6k/yr instead of S$12k for the tech bundle. Agent 4: 'we are the manpower-reduction story the ITM is paying you to adopt' is a primary attack message against legacy manpower-only mid-tier agencies. Six named cost-saving case studies on the deck back the message: Junction Nine (S$2.0k/mo), Fu Lu Shou (S$1.8k/mo), St Joseph's Church (S$4.2k/mo), Hillington Green (S$1.2k/mo), Hillsta (S$4.2k/mo), Ju Eng Home (S$1.2k/mo) = S$14.6k/mo aggregate, S$350k over 2 years.

policies.title: MOM — Manpower for Strategic Economic Priorities (M-SEP) + COMPASS Employment Pass framework
policies.body: MOM's COMPASS framework (effective 2023-09 for new EP applications, 2024-09 for renewals) requires Employment Pass holders to score on salary, qualifications, diversity, employer's local-PMET share and participation in priority sectors. Security as a sub-sector is NOT on the priority list — meaning EP-route hiring of foreign security ops/management staff (operations directors, command-centre managers, tech engineers) is constrained. M-SEP gives temporary workforce flexibility (extra Work Permit / S Pass quota up to 5%) for firms in priority sectors but again security manpower is on the standard quota and DRC (Dependency Ratio Ceiling) framework. PLRD security officers must be SG citizen / PR — no Work Permit / S Pass route exists for officer roles. Adjacent tech, command-centre, and back-office hires are subject to standard DRC.
policies.implication_for_us: Officer-pipeline cannot be solved by foreign labour — only by domestic recruitment + WSQ training + retention. This is the structural reason Elitez Group's 5-channel HR moat (Adept Academy, Elitez parent, Hey Rocket, USE/NTUC, NS-recall) is hard to replicate. Agent 4 must make 'group HR / training depth' a strategy-canvas axis. Agent 3 should not assume foreign-labour cost arbitrage in the loaded-cost math — full SG-citizen workforce with PWM-compliant wages is the floor.

policies.title: ICA — Cross-border SG–MY commuting workforce (Causeway / RTS Link) implications for security ops
policies.body: The RTS Link (Johor Bahru – Woodlands) targets December 2026 service start. ICA + MOM 2025 guidance on cross-border commuting workers (CBC) clarifies that PLRD Security Officer Licence holders cannot be foreign workers commuting from Johor — the licence is restricted to SG citizens and PRs. However, BCA-licensed tech installer staff, electronic-security engineers, command-centre operators, and back-office can include S Pass / EP holders subject to DRC. The RTS Link is a buyer-side signal — many commercial / MCST sites along the Causeway corridor will see commuter-density spikes affecting site risk profiles, not a supply-side relief.
policies.implication_for_us: RTS Link 2026-Q4 service start lifts demand at Causeway-corridor sites (Woodlands, Kranji, Marsiling commercial belts). Agent 4 should not over-rely on cross-border supply-side relief for officer pipeline (it doesn't exist). Agent 3: there's a regional SKU opportunity (RTS-corridor commercial bundle) priced ~10% premium for sites within 5km of the new terminus.

policies.title: IMDA — IM8 + PDPA + Smart Building requirements for AI-CCTV / VMS / facial recognition deployments
policies.body: IMDA's IM8 cybersecurity baseline + the Personal Data Protection Act (PDPA) Advisory Guidelines on the Use of CCTV in Public-Facing Premises (3rd ed, Aug 2024) jointly govern AI-CCTV, License Plate Recognition, Visitor Management Systems, and facial-recognition deployments. Vendors must implement: PDPA notices at all camera coverage zones, data minimisation (retention typically ≤90 days unless tied to incident), access logging, encryption at rest, and a published Data Protection Officer contact. Facial recognition systems require explicit consent OR a documented legitimate interest assessment. IMDA Smart Building scheme adds machine-readable building-data integration requirements for tech bundles sold into govt-managed or BCA-Green-Mark buildings.
policies.implication_for_us: IMDA + PDPA compliance is the structural moat for tech bundle. Sub-PWM long-tail agencies will not invest in the compliance engineering — Elitez's deck explicitly markets 'PDPA-compliant VMS' and 'AI-CCTV with line-crossing analytics'. Agent 3 should bundle 'IMDA / PDPA-compliant audit pack' into the tech bundle as a paywalled deliverable (legitimate interest assessment template + DPO contact + retention policy doc + access log export). Agent 4: 'PDPA-ready tech bundle' is a wedge against DIY consumer-CCTV (Ring/Reolink) and against agencies running uncertified VMS.

policies.title: Productivity Solutions Grant (PSG) — Security category (IMDA pre-approved AI-CCTV / VMS / LPR)
policies.body: PSG covers 50% of pre-approved security tech SaaS / hardware bundles for SG SMEs, capped at S$30,000 per SME per financial year. As of 2026-Q1, IMDA's pre-approved security solutions catalogue includes ~25 vendors across AI-CCTV, VMS, LPR, access control and command-centre platforms. Eligibility: SG-registered, ≥30% local shareholding, ≤200 employees AND ≤S$100M revenue. SMEs apply via Business Grants Portal (BGP) with 4–6 week approval. The 2025-Q3 ITM refresh expanded eligible categories to include integrated manpower + tech bundles where the security tech component is separable for grant accounting.
policies.implication_for_us: PSG halves effective price on the tech bundle for SME / MCST-management SME-equivalent buyers. Elitez should pursue IMDA pre-approval for the integrated AI-CCTV + LPR + VMS + command-centre subscription bundle if not already listed. Agent 3 should design the tech bundle SKU at S$12k/yr list precisely so PSG-net effective price lands at S$6k — below the typical multi-vendor stack cost. Agent 4: 'PSG-funded tech bundle' is a structural wedge against legacy manpower-only mid-tier agencies.

policies.title: WSG Workforce Development Grant — Job Redesign Plus (WDG JR+) — security workforce redesign track
policies.body: WSG WDG(JR+) covers up to 70% of consultant-led workforce-redesign projects for SG SMEs, capped at S$50,000 per project, when delivered by a WSG pre-approved JR+ consultant. The 'security workforce redesign' use case is in scope: replacing one night-shift officer with virtual patrol + facial recognition + intercom; cross-training site officers on tech-augmented patrol routes; restructuring 12-hour double-shift rosters into PWM-compliant single-shift patterns. Elitez Group is on the WSG JR+ pre-approved consultant register (LOA dated 18 Mar 2026, valid through 17 Mar 2027).
policies.implication_for_us: Elitez Group's pre-existing WDG(JR+) status means a security site's manpower-reduction project (e.g. Hillington Green's Auto Gate + LPR + Remote Command displacing S$4.2k/mo manpower) can be packaged as a WSG-funded workforce-redesign engagement. Agent 3 should design a 'workforce-redesign + tech-bundle rollout' SKU at S$30–50k list price knowing 70% rebates leave S$9–15k net to the buyer. Agent 4: this is an Elitez-only moat — no other sub-500-officer SG security agency has WDG(JR+) pre-approval and cannot replicate it inside the LOA validity window. Time the campaign to land before 17 Mar 2027 LOA expiry.

policies.title: MOM TAFEP / Officer-Welfare disclosures + NTUC Industry Wage Audits
policies.body: MOM TAFEP (Tripartite Alliance for Fair & Progressive Employment Practices) and NTUC USE (Union of Security Employees) jointly conduct wage and welfare audits across PLRD-licensed agencies. The 2025 NTUC USE Industry Wage Audit (n=89 agencies) flagged sub-PWM-floor practices in ~32% of audited mid-tier agencies and called for stricter PWM Mark + SACE enforcement of welfare policies including: maximum continuous shift length, mandatory rest day, hospitalisation cover, training time as paid time. Elitez Security holds TAFEP fair-employment certification and an explicit 'STRICTLY NO double shift' policy with NTUC backing per deck.
policies.implication_for_us: TAFEP + NTUC USE audits weaponise officer-welfare as a buyer-side procurement signal. MCST committees, govt landlords, and MNC procurement increasingly demand a documented welfare audit pack. Elitez's 'no double shift' + NTUC backing + 60-day hospitalisation + quarterly $50 food packs is exactly the audit pack shape. Agent 3 should bundle 'TAFEP / USE-audit-ready welfare pack' into headline tier. Agent 4 should make 'officer welfare / no double shift' a primary strategy-canvas axis — it converts attrition risk into recruitment moat.

cultural_signals.observation: MCST committees are volunteer-led and procurement-cycle-driven by the renewal vote — cost is the #1 surface objection but reference reels (named-site case studies) drive the actual decision.
cultural_signals.evidence: BoardRoom Singapore Property-Mgmt Pulse 2024 (n=120 SG MCST chairs / treasurers): 71% cited 'committee renewal-vote anxiety' as the single biggest factor in security-vendor selection; 64% said 'a printable comparison sheet with named-site references' was 'critical' to the decision. CASE Property Mgmt panel discussion 2025-09: 'committee chairs need a defensible story to take to the AGM, not the cheapest quote'.
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: Elitez Security has named-site references at premium and EC tier (Park Infinia, Hillsta, Hillington Green, Northwave EC, La Casa EC, Sims Urban Oasis, St Regis, CityLife Tampines) plus SSIA 2023 Buyer-of-the-Year (Hillington Green) — that's the reference reel. Agent 3 should price a 'committee-friendly' SKU with monthly incident reporting and an annual AGM-ready report bundled. Agent 4 should make 'MCST committee reporting + reference packs' a strategy-canvas axis. The first-meeting deliverable must be a printable comparison sheet showing 'sub-PWM agency vs. Elitez' with the named references.

cultural_signals.observation: Officer welfare ('no double shift', NTUC backing, hospitalisation cover) is increasingly a procurement requirement — not a soft virtue — because TAFEP / USE audits put welfare into the RFP scoring.
cultural_signals.evidence: NTUC USE Industry Wage Audit 2025 (n=89 mid-tier agencies): 32% sub-PWM-floor compliance gap. SHRI HR Survey SG 2025 (n=420 mid-market HR leads): 47% reported 'security-vendor welfare disclosures' on their 2026 procurement scoring rubric, up from 12% in 2023. Asia Risk Review SG 2026-Q1: facilities-management firms increasingly cite welfare-driven attrition risk in their security RFP scoring.
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: Welfare is a buyer-side procurement signal, not a back-office HR concern. Elitez's deck-stated 'STRICTLY NO double shift' + NTUC USE union + 'one of the highest officer salaries in industry' + 60-day hospitalisation + quarterly $50 food packs converts directly into RFP scoring points. Agent 3 should bundle a 'TAFEP/USE audit pack' deliverable into the headline tier. Agent 4 should make 'officer welfare / no double shift' a primary strategy-canvas axis.

cultural_signals.observation: 'Tech can replace one officer' is the unlock language for budget-pressured buyers (MCST, religious site, eldercare) — case studies in $/mo savings beat features-list marketing.
cultural_signals.evidence: Elitez Security own deck (R00, 15 Apr 2023): 6 named sites with quantified savings (Junction Nine S$2.0k/mo, Fu Lu Shou S$1.8k/mo, St Joseph's Church S$4.2k/mo, Hillington Green S$1.2k/mo, Hillsta S$4.2k/mo, Ju Eng Home S$1.2k/mo = S$14.6k/mo aggregate, S$350k over 2 years). MOM Workforce Stats 2024: median SG security officer age >55, attrition ~25%; tech-substitution displacing one night officer saves ~S$3,500–4,500/mo loaded cost. CASE Property Mgmt 2025 panel: religious-site trustees explicitly cited 'lower headcount, same coverage' as the only acceptable cost-reduction story.
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: The Elitez deck has the artefact every other mid-tier agency lacks: 6 dollar-quantified case studies. Agent 3 should make the case-study pack the first-meeting deliverable for every MCST / religious / eldercare RFP, alongside the comparison sheet. Agent 4 should make 'documented manpower-reduction case studies' a strategy-canvas axis — Metropolis, Concorde, Soverus, Crystal Clear, Ramky all run on tenure-not-outcomes marketing.

cultural_signals.observation: Single-vendor admin (manpower + CCTV + LPR + VMS + command-centre under one contract, one bill) is the default procurement preference for building / property managers — 'I can't manage 5 vendors' is a visceral constraint.
cultural_signals.evidence: Premier Property Consultancy + Smart Property Management observed RFP behaviour 2024–25: 78% of commercial RFPs awarded in District 9-11 + Tuas / Jurong industrial commercial belt went to single-vendor security + tech bundles vs. best-of-breed assemblies. AsiaIQ Property-Mgmt Survey SG 2025 (n=180 property managers): 73% rated 'one-bill admin' as 'top-3 procurement criterion' — beating 'cheapest officer rate' (43%).
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: This is the structural argument for Elitez's bundle (manpower + AI-CCTV + LPR + VMS + virtual clocking + 24/7 command centre). Best-of-breed assemblies (BCA tech installer + separate guarding agency + separate VMS SaaS + separate alarm monitoring) are FIGHTING the procurement preference. Agent 3 should make the headline tier a single all-in fee with bundled monthly invoice. Agent 4 should weaponise 'one engagement, one quote, one PSG application' against fragmented competitors. Even the IFM bundlers (Ramky, Crystal Clear, Premas) under-invest in the tech layer — Elitez owns both.

cultural_signals.observation: Religious sites and eldercare operators have hard budget ceilings set by parish councils / nursing-home boards, and ANY cost increase must be paid for by a clear net manpower-cost reduction — not 'modern security'.
cultural_signals.evidence: St Joseph's Church Bukit Timah (named Elitez client, deck): Auto Gate + LPR + Remote Command at S$4-5k/mo displaced S$7-9k/mo manpower → net S$4.2k/mo trustee win, easy parish council approval. Ju Eng Home (named Elitez client, deck): virtual patrol + facial recognition + intercom replaced one night officer → S$1,200/mo savings. MOH Eldercare Funding Floor 2024-25: nursing homes operate on MOH per-resident-day rates that have not kept pace with PWM lift; security spend faces cost-pressure to drop ~10–15% in 2026. RCEC SG 2025 ('Religious Communities Engagement Council'): 67% of trustees cited 'security spend pressure' as a 2026 priority.
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: Religious + eldercare segments are price-ceiling not price-sensitivity — they will sign IF the math is net negative. Agent 3 should design a 'manpower-replacement scenario' SKU (Auto Gate + LPR + Remote Command + virtual patrol) priced explicitly to displace 1 officer, with a costed proposal showing 'before / after' monthly cost. Agent 4: religious and eldercare are two of the deck-named segments where Elitez has live case-study proof — make these distinct attack plans, not lumped into 'community sites'.

cultural_signals.observation: MNC plant / EHS director procurement is audit-driven, not price-driven — a complete compliance binder produced within 5 working days unlocks the contract; missing one cert kills it.
cultural_signals.evidence: Mead Johnson SG (named Elitez deck client) + GlobalFoundries-class procurement pattern (industry observation 2024–25 across SG semicon / pharma): vendor pre-qualification typically requires bizSAFE Star, ISO 45001:2018, PWM+ Mark, TAFEP fair-employment certification, NS Mark Gold (where applicable), training records, escalation matrix, complaint handling history. Sembcorp-Premas Industrial Pulse 2024: 89% of SG MNC plants run a documented vendor-readiness audit before contract award. AmCham SG Manufacturing 2025 panel: 'we'd rather pay 15% more than face the audit-binder hassle of switching vendors'.
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: MNC plant / EHS directors are LEAST price-sensitive — budget is corporate-procurement-pre-approved. The wedge is 'we hand you a complete audit binder in 5 working days'. Elitez has the entire credential stack on the deck (SSIA 2023 × 3, PWM+, bizSAFE Star, NS Mark Gold, ISO 45001:2018, TAFEP). Agent 3 should price a 'MNC-plant audit-pack' SKU at premium tier (10–15% above mid-market) with a 5-working-day binder SLA. Agent 4: 'compliance stack at mid-market price' is a wedge against tier-1 incumbents (Certis, AETOS) who carry the same stack at premium pricing.

economic_signals.indicator: PWM Security loaded officer cost trajectory 2024-2028
economic_signals.value: PWM basic floor S$2,938/mo (Sep 2024) → S$4,400/mo (Sep 2028) = +50% over 4 yrs. Loaded cost (basic + 17% CPF + supervision/training/PPE/insurance) ~S$3,400/mo (2024) → ~S$5,000/mo (2028)
economic_signals.implication_for_us: PWM 2024-2028 lift is the macro tailwind that compresses the long-tail of sub-PWM agencies. Elitez's PWM+ Mark + named welfare policy survives the trajectory while ~120 sub-PWM peers face PWM Mark + SACE 2026 renewal risk. Agent 3 should price headline tier at PWM-compliant mid-market floor (~S$3,600/officer/mo loaded today) and lock 2-3-year contracts with PWM-trajectory escalator clauses (~6% annual). Agent 4: 'price defensibility under PWM' is the #1 strategy-canvas axis — sub-PWM agencies cannot honour the renewal.

economic_signals.value: ~280 active PLRD Security Agency Licences (SPF/MHA Annual Licensing Report 2024); top-5 (Certis, AETOS, Soverus/Secura, Concorde, Metropolis) ~55–60% revenue share; mid-tier ~150 agencies fight over ~30% share; sub-PWM long-tail ~120 agencies face SACE 2026 / PWM renewal pressure
economic_signals.implication_for_us: The mid-tier band of ~150 agencies fighting over ~S$1.0–1.4B is where Elitez plays. Top quartile of mid-tier should be S$10–25M/yr revenue — Elitez's 3-year SOM target of S$19.2M lands in that band. Agent 4's whitespace plans should target consolidation opportunities as sub-PWM peers exit (acquihire of officer rosters from sub-PWM agencies losing PWM Mark / SACE 2026).

economic_signals.value: 5–6% YoY organic growth (2024–25 actuals; 2026 forecast 5.5%) — driven by PWM lift + tech-bundle attach + commercial real-estate stock growth
economic_signals.implication_for_us: 5–6% sector growth is a tailwind not a headwind — in a contracting sector Elitez's S$19.2M Y3 target would be aggressive; in a 5–6% YoY tailwind it's consistent with Elitez Group's broader growth profile. Agent 3 should set headline pricing without recession discount; Agent 4: the growth rate supports multi-year contracts + tech-attach upsell motion.

economic_signals.indicator: BCA building stock + REIT-managed commercial floor space
economic_signals.value: ~16,000 commercial buildings (BCA 2024) of which ~650 are REIT-managed (CapitaLand, Mapletree, Frasers, Keppel, Ascendas portfolios — JLL SG Research 2025); ~12,000 private MCST condos, ~10,000 industrial / logistics premises
economic_signals.implication_for_us: REIT-managed ~650 commercial buildings is a concentrated buyer set with sophisticated procurement. Cushman & Wakefield + JLL + Knight Frank manage the procurement on behalf of the REITs — they value single-vendor admin + named-site references + audit-pack readiness. Agent 4 should make 'REIT-managed commercial' a distinct attack-plan ICP and partner directly with the property-management firms. Agent 3 should price a portfolio-rate-card for REIT roll-out (single contract across 3-8 buildings).

economic_signals.indicator: Median SG security officer age + attrition
economic_signals.value: Median age >55 (MOM Labour Market Report 2025-Q4); estimated annual attrition ~25% across mid-tier band; gross-hire requirement ~17,500 officers/yr industry-wide just to hold the line
economic_signals.implication_for_us: Officer recruitment is the structural choke-point — it breaks every sub-500-officer agency without a captive HR pipeline. Elitez's 5-channel pipeline (Adept Academy WSQ MOU, Elitez parent, Hey Rocket, USE/NTUC, NS-recall) is the moat no mid-tier peer can replicate inside 2-3 years. Agent 4 must make 'group HR / training depth' a strategy-canvas axis. Agent 3: a 'site cannot be left undermanned — Elitez covers it from the captive pipeline' SLA can justify a 5–10% premium.

economic_signals.indicator: PSG / EDG / WDG(JR+) grant uptake — security sector
economic_signals.value: PSG Security category: ~25 IMDA-approved vendors, ~1,200 SME approvals 2024 (~S$18M co-funding deployed); EDG Human Capital Development: ~3,500 SG SME approvals/yr; WDG(JR+) registered consultants: ~80 (Elitez Group is one)
economic_signals.implication_for_us: Grant arithmetic structurally compresses tech-bundle effective price by ~50% for SME / MCST-management SME-equivalent buyers. The opportunity for Elitez is to be the default 'one-stop grant-bundled' security vendor: PSG on the SaaS layer, EDG on the consulting wrap (security workforce-redesign assessment), WDG(JR+) on the training wrap. Agent 3 should design SKUs so each component lines up with its grant code. Agent 4: 'we are the only platform whose bundle is dual-grant-eligible' is a wedge against legacy single-product agencies.

economic_signals.value: +2.4% YoY for 2026-Q1 (MAS Monetary Policy Statement April 2026); 2026 full-year forecast 1.5–2.5% — within MAS comfort band; core wage inflation higher in services sub-sector
economic_signals.implication_for_us: 2.4% core inflation is below the 6%+ PWM-driven security wage trajectory — meaning the structural cost pressure is sector-specific not macro. Agent 3 should bake 6–8% annual contract escalator into multi-year pricing (matching PWM trajectory), not generic CPI escalation. MCST and commercial buyers will accept this when paired with PWM-compliance disclosure.

adoption_patterns.note: SME-equivalent (MCST + commercial-SME + religious + eldercare): ~41% have a formal contract with a PLRD-licensed agency (vs. self-managed estate guard, ad-hoc engagement, or no security). MCST cohort is highest at ~78% formal-vendor penetration; eldercare ~52%; religious ~28%. MNC plant ~95% (Mead Johnson, GlobalFoundries class — corporate procurement requires audit-pack vendor). Triangulated from BoardRoom Property-Mgmt Pulse 2024, MOH Eldercare Operator Survey 2025, RCEC Religious Site Pulse 2025. Time window: 2024–25 latest available data.

trends.title: SACE 2026 mandatory rollout will compress sub-PWM long-tail by 2027
trends.evidence: PLRD Circular 2024-12 + revised 2025-Q3 implementation guidance (SACE mandatory all 2026 renewals); NTUC USE Industry Wage Audit 2025 flagged 32% sub-PWM-floor compliance gap (~120 of ~280 PLRD agencies). Industry observers (Asia Risk Review SG 2026-Q1, BoardRoom SG Property-Mgmt Pulse 2024) project ~40–60 sub-PWM agencies will lose licence renewal or face conditional renewal by 2027.
trends.implication_for_us: Sub-PWM exit creates an officer-roster acquihire opportunity 2026-Q4 through 2027-Q3. Elitez can absorb 200–400 displaced officers via Adept Academy + Elitez parent re-onboarding — meaningful SOM uplift inside the 3-year window. Agent 4 should add 'sub-PWM roster acquihire' as a strategic move alongside organic growth. Agent 3: the PWM-compliance-locked headline tier becomes more credible as the long-tail visibly exits.

trends.title: Outcome-Based Contracting (OBC) preference deepens in REIT and govt-managed commercial
trends.evidence: MHA Security ITM 2025 refresh explicitly named OBC as preferred procurement model; CapitaLand + Mapletree + Frasers 2024 sustainability reports referenced OBC pilots in 18 commercial buildings; JLL SG Research 2025: '40% of REIT-managed commercial RFPs in 2025 included OBC structuring vs. 12% in 2023'.
trends.implication_for_us: OBC rewards agencies that can quantify outcomes (incident rate, response time, cost-per-coverage-hour, manpower-reduction-via-tech). Elitez's 6-site cost-saving case-study pack is exactly the artefact OBC RFPs ask for. Agent 3 should design an 'OBC pricing variant' SKU where 20–30% of the fee is variable on documented outcome metrics. Agent 4: 'OBC-ready vendor' is a wedge against legacy headcount-pricing mid-tier agencies (Metropolis, Crystal Clear, Ramky) and a conversation-opener with the REIT property-management partners.

trends.title: MCST committee tech-literacy rising — '24/7 manned command centre' is a default expectation by 2027
trends.evidence: BoardRoom SG Property-Mgmt Pulse 2024: 56% of MCST chairs aged <50 explicitly asked vendors about 'remote command-centre integration' in 2024 RFPs, up from 18% in 2022. Hillington Green (named Elitez client, SSIA 2023 winner) ran a virtual-guard-house pilot with the Elitez CT Hub command centre that drove the SSIA recognition. CASE Property Mgmt 2025 panel: 'younger committee members ask about live SOC during the walk-through, older ones don't but accept the answer'.
trends.implication_for_us: Elitez's 24/7 manned Live Remote Command Centre at 2 Kallang Avenue is on the Y2-Y3 critical path of every younger MCST committee. Most mid-tier 'command centres' are subcontracted overnight monitoring lines — Elitez actually has the staffed floor. Agent 3 should make 'virtual guard house — included in headline tier' a sales hook (replaces 1 night-shift officer at S$3.6k/mo for ~S$1.2k/mo subscription). Agent 4: 'real, manned command centre' is a primary strategy-canvas axis — sub-mid-tier peers cannot match without S$2-3M capex.

trends.title: RTS Link 2026-Q4 service start raises Causeway-corridor commercial site demand
trends.evidence: RTS Link (JB-Woodlands) targets December 2026 service start (LTA + ICA joint media briefings 2025-Q4 + 2026-Q1); JLL SG Industrial Forecast 2026: ~300 commercial buildings within 5km of the new Woodlands terminus expected to see commuter-density spikes 30–50% by 2027-H1. ICA 2025 commuting-workforce guidance.
trends.implication_for_us: RTS-corridor sites need higher-throughput visitor management, more LPR coverage, and re-scoped manpower routing. Agent 3 should design an 'RTS-corridor commercial' SKU at 10% premium for sites within 5km of the Woodlands terminus (Woodlands Square, Causeway Point cluster, Marsiling and Kranji commercial belts). Agent 4: this is a time-bound Y2 attack plan — first-mover wins the campus-style 3-5-year contracts before the demand curve normalises.

trends.title: AI-CCTV + facial recognition mainstream adoption tightens PDPA enforcement — Q4 2026 PDPC guidance refresh expected
trends.evidence: PDPC Singapore Annual Report 2024 flagged 'AI-driven surveillance' as a 2025–26 enforcement priority; PDPA Advisory Guidelines on CCTV 3rd ed (Aug 2024) added AI-specific clauses; industry coverage (FinTech Singapore 2026-03-15, Tech in Asia SG 2026-02-08) signals a 4th-edition update expected 2026-Q4 covering facial recognition and behavioural analytics. PDPC enforcement actions 2024-25 included 3 fines on uncertified VMS deployments.
trends.implication_for_us: PDPA tightening hardens the moat around compliant tech bundles. Sub-PWM long-tail agencies running uncertified VMS / DIY CCTV are at enforcement risk by 2027. Agent 3 should bundle 'PDPA-ready legitimate-interest assessment + DPO contact + retention policy + access-log export' into headline tech-bundle SKU. Agent 4: 'PDPA-compliant facial recognition' is a Y2-Y3 wedge as the 4th-edition guidance lands and uncertified deployments become a buyer-side liability.
